questions.question: 中文导读：In Bayes' theorem, what is the 'prior'?
options: 中文选项：The probability of the evidence
中文选项：Your belief about a hypothesis before observing any evidence
中文选项：The probability of the evidence given the hypothesis
中文选项：The final updated probability after seeing evidence
questions.explanation: 中文解析：The prior P(A) represents your initial belief about hypothesis A before seeing any data. It gets updated to the posterior P(A|B) after observing evidence B.

questions.question: 中文导读：A rare disease affects 1 in 10,000 people. A 99% accurate test returns positive. What is the approximate probability you have the disease?
options: 中文选项：99%
中文选项：About 1%
中文选项：50%
中文选项：10%
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Despite 99% test accuracy, the disease is so rare (0.01%) that most positive results come from healthy people (false positives). Bayes' theorem gives P(sick|positive) = ~0.98%, not 99%.

questions.question: 中文导读：What is Laplace smoothing in a Naive Bayes classifier and why is it necessary?
options: 中文选项：It normalizes feature values to have zero mean and unit variance
中文选项：It adds a small count to every feature to prevent zero probabilities from unseen words, which would zero out the entire product
中文选项：It smooths the decision boundary between classes
中文选项：It reduces the dimensionality of the feature space
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Without smoothing, a word never seen in spam training data gets P(word|spam)=0, making the entire product zero regardless of other strong spam indicators. Adding 1 to each count prevents this.

questions.question: 中文导读：How does MAP (Maximum A Posteriori) estimation differ from MLE (Maximum Likelihood Estimation)?
options: 中文选项：MAP uses a larger dataset than MLE
中文选项：MAP incorporates a prior distribution over parameters, equivalent to adding regularization, while MLE only uses the likelihood
中文选项：MAP is faster to compute than MLE
中文选项：MLE always produces better results than MAP
questions.explanation: 中文解析：MAP maximizes P(data|params) * P(params) while MLE maximizes P(data|params) alone. The prior P(params) acts as regularization -- a Gaussian prior equals L2 regularization, a Laplace prior equals L1.

questions.question: 中文导读：In sequential Bayesian updating, you start with Beta(1,1) and observe 7 heads and 3 tails. What is the posterior?
options: 中文选项：Beta(7, 3)
中文选项：Beta(8, 4)
中文选项：Beta(1.7, 1.3)
中文选项：Normal(0.7, 0.1)
questions.explanation: 中文解析：The Beta-Binomial conjugate update adds successes to the first parameter and failures to the second: Beta(1+7, 1+3) = Beta(8, 4). The posterior mean is 8/12 = 0.667.
